task_completion.description: Open the chinook.odb database in LibreOffice Base
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreOffice Base window is open with 'chinook.odb' visible in the title bar or the database structure (Tables, Queries, etc.) is loaded.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the PlaylistTrack table or SQL execution window
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent opens the 'PlaylistTrack' table in the GUI, OR opens the 'Tools > SQL' window to prepare a deletion command.

task_completion.description: Delete the 26 track associations for PlaylistId 17
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent deleting rows where PlaylistId is 17 in the 'PlaylistTrack' table (e.g., selecting rows and showing a deletion confirmation dialog), OR a 'Command successfully executed' message after running a SQL DELETE statement targeting PlaylistTrack.

task_completion.description: Delete the 'Heavy Metal Classic' playlist record
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent deleting the row for 'Heavy Metal Classic' (PlaylistId 17) in the 'Playlist' table, OR a 'Command successfully executed' message after running a SQL DELETE statement targeting the Playlist table.
The agent clicks the Save icon (floppy disk) in the main LibreOffice Base window, uses File -> Save, or confirms saving changes when prompted upon closing the application.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Base application to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: The LibreOffice Base GUI is visible in the screenshots, rather than the agent attempting to modify the .odb file with a text editor or unsupported terminal tool.

integrity.description: The agent deleted the child records before the parent record
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent deletes the records in the 'PlaylistTrack' table BEFORE deleting the record in the 'Playlist' table, or uses a single SQL transaction that handles both correctly.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed the deletions
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show actual database modification actions (like executing SQL commands or confirming GUI row deletions) rather than just viewing the tables or hiding rows.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The database is an ODB file located at /home/ga/chinook.odb. The target playlist is 'Heavy Metal Classic' (PlaylistId = 17). There are exactly 26 track associations in the PlaylistTrack table for PlaylistId = 17. To maintain referential integrity, the 26 PlaylistTrack records must be deleted before deleting the Playlist record.
